checks.finding: T-001 Risk: low is consistent with a docs-only prose/table-append scope with no control-flow, data, or security impact (tasks.md:111-125). T-002 Risk: medium is consistent with a data-only edit inside an existing schema, no sensitive surface (auth/payment/PII/migration/public-API-shape), external mechanical verification (tasks.md:273-292). T-003 Risk: high is consistent with sentinel surfaces from risk-classification-policy.md: a scheduled CI job holding issues: write that processes untrusted external content into a publicly-posted issue body, plus the spec's one protected-enforcement-chain file touch (tasks.md:445-466, security-spec.md STRIDE B1/B3). No over- or under-classification found.

T-003 is the only Risk: high/critical task. Its Done When contains explicit Red/Green-separated TDD evidence ('RED -- the authored suite run against the pre-landing tree ... failing meaningfully; GREEN -- the post-commit-A run of every bundle above passing', tasks.md:685-691) and an independent-verdict item ('An independent quality-gate verdict records PASS for this task', tasks.md:691-692), consistent with this SDD framework's lifecycle rule that only quality-gate (not implement-task) may set Done (tasks.md:15-17). No Risk: critical task exists, so second-approver/signed-evidence-bundle items do not apply.

Re-verified against the current tasks.md text (round-2 remediation). T-003's Done When now has exactly 7 bundled items (confirmed by direct enumeration: Fixture-driven fail-soft/divergence bundle, Branch-completeness bundle, Self-consistency bundle, Protected-file boundary bundle, One-time manual verification, Shared legs bundle, TDD evidence -- tasks.md:640-692), down from the round-1-flagged 13, while still citing every TEST ID the traceability Task Mapping assigns to T-003 (TEST-005/006/007/008/009/010/011/016/018/019/020/021 -- traceability.md:30). Scope's Commit A (tasks.md:564-621) now opens with an explicit single-session coherence argument: the fixture/stub harness is built once in stage (i) and shared by every script-driving assertion, stage (v) is a mechanical cp+hash, and AC-008 is not code work at all. T-001 (8 bullets) and T-002 (7 bullets) are both appropriately sized. No fragmentation indicators (no 3+ single-function tasks, no bare rename-only task).

T-003 (the primary behavioral task) has extensive edge/error-path AC coverage: AC-006 (three fetch-failure scenarios), AC-007 (divergence + dedup negative branch), AC-020 (no-diff/zero-side-effect branch), AC-021 (adversarial payload / issue-body trust boundary) -- acceptance-tests.md:15-16,29-30. T-002 has AC-013, a non-mutation/hash-comparison guard against unintended side effects on the frozen v1 registry (acceptance-tests.md:22). T-001 is pure prose/table-append documentation with no executable behavior or failure path (AC-015 confirms host-neutral, no-branch content, acceptance-tests.md:24); per reviewer-calibration.md's false-positive guard against inventing edge-case requirements for content-only, fully-inspectable Done-When outcomes, no error-path test task is required for T-001.

Declared test types align with their actual scope: TEST-006/007/020/021 are labeled 'integration (fixture-driven, real script)' and genuinely exercise >=2 components (real check-model-freshness.sh + stubbed gh wrapper + fixture files, acceptance-tests.md:15-16,29-30). TEST-005/010/013 target a single artifact each (single-module scope: workflow YAML text-marker, a grep against one script, a hash of one file -- acceptance-tests.md:14,19,22). TEST-001..004/012/015/018/019 are 'document conformance' checks each mapped to a specific AC-NNN, matching the acceptance-test scope criterion despite the non-canonical label. TEST-014 is 'existing-suite regression', a distinct, well-defined category this repo consistently uses. No mismatch between declared type and actual scope found.

T-003 (Risk: high) carries an explicit Rollback: field -- 'reviewed revert of this task's commits PLUS a second human-copy application reverting .github/workflows/test.yml's registration line ... the same human-in-the-loop mechanism that added it' (tasks.md:510-514), consistent with infra-spec.md Rollback. No Risk: critical task exists in this spec.

tests/run-all.sh/.ps1 and .github/workflows/test.yml are each touched by exactly one task (T-003 only; Global Constraints explicitly notes no cross-task registration collision, tasks.md:65-68). contracts/agent-model-capabilities.v2.json is written only by T-002; T-003 reads it read-only by construction (security-spec.md B2). CHANGELOG.md is touched by all three tasks but explicitly reasoned as three independent, non-colliding '## Unreleased' entries citing distinct issue numbers with no create-then-append serialization needed (tasks.md:60-64). docs/agent-capability-matrix.md is edited unconditionally by T-001 and only conditionally by T-002 ('gets its confirmation-date column VALUES only if T-001 has landed and a genuine reference exists', tasks.md:381-384) -- this is a self-aware, order-tolerant conditional (verify-and-leave-unchanged is the explicit default per the Deliverables table, traceability.md:63) rather than an uncoordinated simultaneous-purpose overlap.

T-003's Blockers: T-001 is a genuine content dependency (D2's filed-issue body must cite the identical canonical-source list and [model-freshness-divergence] marker literal T-001 documents, avoiding two divergent copies -- tasks.md:477-481, Global Constraints tasks.md:77-84). T-002's external Pillar-C precondition is correctly kept out of the in-spec Blockers field and instead documented as an External Blocker re-verified at task start (requirements.md Field Definitions, Main Workflows item 2) -- this is a deliberate, well-reasoned distinction, not an omission. T-002's conditional (non-forced) touch on docs/agent-capability-matrix.md degrades gracefully to a no-op if T-001 hasn't landed (see SCOPE-DISJOINT), so its absence from Blockers does not risk an integration failure. No spurious or missing blocker found.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. T-001/T-002/T-003 are all net-new feature/documentation/data-population additions (tasks.md T-001/T-002/T-003 Goal sections); none is scoped as a bugfix, regression fix, debugging, or incident-remediation task.
